intro: Ayenia capillata, also known as Capuchina, is a plant species in the family Malpighiaceae, commonly found in the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The bioactive constituents of Ayenia capillata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Ayenia capillata include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
